chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Now, just like we have methods, we also have query selectors.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And the methods we have talked about till now, getElementById, getElementByClassName, getElementByTagName,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: they are used quite frequently. But our query selectors are used more frequently than them.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Because using them and their syntax is more intuitive and easier.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So whenever we have to select an element, generally we will prefer our query selectors.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Query selectors are very similar to our CSS selectors.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: We simply have to write document.querySelector.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And then what we can do, just like we used to use selectors in CSS, we can use all those selectors here as well.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Meaning, in this method, if we have to select something with a tag name, we can simply pass the tag name.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we have to select something with an id, we can write the name of our hash and id.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we have to select a class, we can write the name of our dot and then our class.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: In fact, just like we talked about pseudo elements in CSS selectors.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: For example, let's suppose we have written a class name and then we want to select its nth of type.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we can write nth of type and here we can write n plus 1 or 2n plus 1.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So in this way, the big selector that we used to write in CSS, it is also possible to write it in this query selector.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So basically, the way selectors were used in CSS, we can use it in JavaScript as well.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So let's test all the things out.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: In app.js, first of all, we are going to write document.querySelector and if we pass our h1 tag in the query selector,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: then what will happen is that our h1 element will be selected.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we want, we can print it along with console.dir and here we will print it.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Print, save, refresh.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So this whole object has been printed.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: In the same way, if we want, we can also select it on the basis of our ID.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we can select hash.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: We had created a paragraph whose ID was here.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Equal to description. So let's copy and paste it here.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Save, refresh.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we have our paragraph whose ID is description.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we want, we can also select it from the class name.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Now one thing to note here is that we don't have to remember the names of different methods.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: We just have to remember one method, querySelector, and the way we used selectors in CSS, we have to use it in a similar manner.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Here we can write the name of our class, oldImage.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: OldImage, the class name we used.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Save, refresh.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So what happened, we have image.oldImage.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Now one thing we will note here is that when we used our query selector for our class name,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: here we didn't print collection, we printed a single object.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So this is a slight difference between query selector and our normal methods.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Query selector is used to select only a single element.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So basically the first object, the first element that JavaScript got, whose class name will be oldImage,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: that single element has been selected here.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So the object that has been selected here is the first image.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So what query selector does is that whatever value we pass, it matches all the objects.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: The first object that matches, it returns it to us.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So if multiple matches are available, like let's suppose we pass a paragraph in query selector.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we have two paragraphs available, but our query selector will bring us the first paragraph.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And which paragraph will it give in the first paragraph?

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we go above it, we will get this paragraph, in which our entire starting text is written.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Now if we want, we can also nest in this, let's suppose in index.html.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: What we have done is, in our box div, we have made an unordered list and we have made a list in it.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: We want all our anchor tags in the div, meaning if we want to select only the anchor tags in this div,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: then we can write in query selector for that.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Let's comment this out.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: We can write console.dir document.queryselector.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And in query selector, we can pass all the anchor tags in the div.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we have anchor tags outside the div as well, but if we want only the ones inside the div,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: then we can write similar syntax in JavaScript as we write in CSS selectors.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: We will save and refresh.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: What do we have? We have a.box link.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Basically, the first anchor tag whose class is box link.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: It went to index.html, went inside the div and selected the first anchor tag.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And we have a.box link as the class of anchor tag.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And in its value, we will get a publisher.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So when we scroll it, we will analyze its inner text.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we would have got a publisher in the inner text.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Now this is the way to select a single element with the help of query selector.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: But if we want all the anchor tags in the div,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: basically we want the whole html collection,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: then we have another method for that which is query selector all.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: All will give us all the things.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Query selector gives a single value and all gives the html collection.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Like we want all the anchor tags in the div.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Basically, we just got a single anchor tag.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we want all the anchor tags, then we can do query selector all.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Save. Refresh.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Now we have a node list.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Node list means we talked about trees.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Every individual object has a node.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we got a list of nodes.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And there are 4 nodes in it.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: This is our first anchor tag, this is second, third and fourth anchor tag.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: If we want, we can also talk about its length.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So this is basically a node list that we get.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Similarly, if we want to select all the paragraphs,

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: then we can do query selector all in console.dir.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Save. Refresh.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So we got the first node list, which has a size of 2.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And in this, the first paragraph is selected.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: And this is our second paragraph.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: So there are multiple ways to select objects in any html document.

chunks.title: Query Selectors
chunks.text: Out of these, we will use query selector and query selector all the most frequently.

If we have to select a class, we can write the name of our dot and then our class. In fact, just like we talked about pseudo elements in CSS selectors. For example, let's suppose we have written a class name and then we want to select its nth of type. So we can write nth of type and here we can write n plus 1 or 2n plus 1. So in this way, the big selector that we used to write in CSS, it is also possible to write it in this query selector. So basically, the way selectors were used in CSS, we can use it in JavaScript as well. So let's test all the things out. In app.js, first of all, we are going to write document.querySelector and if we pass our h1 tag in the query selector, then what will happen is that our h1 element will be selected. Now one thing to note here is that we don't have to remember the names of different methods. We just have to remember one method, querySelector, and the way we used selectors in CSS, we have to use it in a similar manner. Here we can write the name of our class, oldImage. OldImage, the class name we used. Save, refresh. So what happened, we have image.oldImage. Now one thing we will note here is that when we used our query selector for our class name, here we didn't print collection, we printed a single object. So this is a slight difference between query selector and our normal methods. Query selector is used to select only a single element. So basically the first object, the first element that JavaScript got, whose class name will be oldImage, that single element has been selected here. So the object that has been selected here is the first image. So what query selector does is that whatever value we pass, it matches all the objects. The first object that matches, it returns it to us. So if multiple matches are available, like let's suppose we pass a paragraph in query selector. So we have two paragraphs available, but our query selector will bring us the first paragraph. And which paragraph will it give in the first paragraph? What we have done is, in our box div, we have made an unordered list and we have made a list in it. We want all our anchor tags in the div, meaning if we want to select only the anchor tags in this div, then we can write in query selector for that. Let's comment this out. We can write console.dir document.queryselector. And in query selector, we can pass all the anchor tags in the div. So we have anchor tags outside the div as well, but if we want only the ones inside the div, then we can write similar syntax in JavaScript as we write in CSS selectors. We will save and refresh. What do we have? We have a.box link. Basically, the first anchor tag whose class is box link. It went to index.html, went inside the div and selected the first anchor tag. And we have a.box link as the class of anchor tag. And in its value, we will get a publisher. So when we scroll it, we will analyze its inner text. So we would have got a publisher in the inner text. Now this is the way to select a single element with the help of query selector. But if we want all the anchor tags in the div, basically we want the whole html collection, then we have another method for that which is query selector all. All will give us all the things. Query selector gives a single value and all gives the html collection. Like we want all the anchor tags in the div. Basically, we just got a single anchor tag. If we want all the anchor tags, then we can do query selector all. Save. Refresh. Now we have a node list. Node list means we talked about trees. Every individual object has a node. So we got a list of nodes. And there are 4 nodes in it. This is our first anchor tag, this is second, third and fourth anchor tag. If we want, we can also talk about its length. So this is basically a node list that we get. Similarly, if we want to select all the paragraphs, then we can do query selector all in console.dir. Save. Refresh. So we got the first node list, which has a size of 2. And in this, the first paragraph is selected. And this is our second paragraph. So there are multiple ways to select objects in any html document. Out of these, we will use query selector and query selector all the most frequently.
